context.description: The system implements authentication on the frontend, but protection is mainly handled at the client-side. Protected pages (e.g., studio, bookmarks) rely on client-side checks after hydration rather than server-side guards. Backend APIs are currently protected via authentication middleware, but frontend route access is not consistently enforced at the server level.

problem.summary: Users can access protected routes at the UI level without server-side enforcement, and authentication checks occur only after client-side hydration.

details.issue: Protected pages are rendered without server-side redirect or middleware guard.
Authentication and role checks are performed in client components after hydration.
Users without valid authentication can still load the page shell before being blocked by UI logic.
No Next.js middleware or server-side session validation is applied to route access.
Auth state relies on localStorage, which is not accessible at the server level.

problem.expected_behavior: Protected routes should be enforced at the server level, preventing unauthenticated users from accessing page content at all (via middleware or server-side redirects). Authentication should be validated before rendering protected pages.
problem.actual_behavior: Protected pages are accessible initially and only restricted on the client side after hydration, resulting in weak route-level protection (UX-level only).

possible_causes.cause: Missing Next.js middleware for route protection
Lack of server-side session/token validation for protected pages
Authentication logic implemented only in client components
Reliance on localStorage for auth state, which is not accessible in server components or middleware
No centralized route guard mechanism for frontend routes

requirements.requirement: Identify all protected routes (e.g., studio, bookmarks)
Check whether any server-side middleware or redirect logic exists
Confirm authentication checks occur only after client hydration
Add Next.js middleware to protect sensitive routes
Redirect unauthenticated users before page render
Ensure protected routes cannot be accessed without valid session/token
Migrate from localStorage-based auth to cookie-based or server-readable session
Ensure tokens are accessible in middleware/server components
Ensure all sensitive endpoints use authentication middleware (e.g., authenticateUser)
Attempt direct API calls using Postman/curl without authentication
Attempt calls with valid token but insufficient permissions
Verify backend returns appropriate HTTP status codes (401/403)
Log unauthorized access attempts to protected routes
Test unauthenticated access to protected routes
Test direct API access bypass scenarios

acceptance_criteria.criteria: Protected routes are inaccessible without authentication at server level
Unauthenticated users are redirected before page render
All backend APIs enforce authentication and authorization
No direct API bypass is possible
Auth mechanism is consistent across frontend and backend
